title: Hackathon Presentation Slides
description: Slide deck from the GPU-accelerated ACTUS hackathon — problem statement, solution design, architecture, and benchmark results.

title: Documentation Site Walkthrough
description: Video guide demonstrating how to use the ACTUS Insurance documentation site, including navigation, resource downloads, and interactive features.

title: PAM Test Data (json)
description: All 42 PAM test cases in JSON format — input terms, expected cash flows, and state vectors per event type.

title: Benchmark Results and Data
description: Comprehensive benchmark results, including raw data files, analysis scripts, and visualizations comparing CPU and GPU performance across all test cases. Used for the gpu vs cpu comparison in the hackathon presentation.

title: The Hackathon Story — Full Documentation (PDF)
description: Follow the journey from a single-threaded ACTUS prototype to a massively parallel GPU engine — covering the motivation, architecture decisions, trade-offs, and benchmark results that defined the project

title: ACTUS Organization — Full Documentation (PDF)
description: Understand the ACTUS open standard — its contract taxonomy of ~32 types, data dictionary, state transition functions, business rules, and the foundation that maintains the specification

title: Background — GPU Computing — Full Documentation (PDF)
description: Build intuition for why GPUs are a natural fit for bulk financial contract evaluation — comparing CPU and GPU architectures, and introducing ILGPU as the bridge from C# to CUDA and OpenCL

title: Technical Documentation — Full Documentation (PDF)
description: Deep-dive into the implementation — the PAM contract engine, state machine, schedule generation, risk factor model, GPU data structures and kernels, sample demos, and the full test suite

title: ACTUS Insurance Extensions — Full Documentation (PDF)
description: Explore how ACTUS was extended into insurance — projecting life insurance cashflows on GPU using Markov state transitions and a domain-specific language that encodes product rules without code changes
